stories.title: Why the Ravens Waived QB Diego Pavia, and What Could Be Next for Former Heisman Finalist
stories.summary: The Baltimore Ravens waived quarterback Diego Pavia on Thursday, clearing roster space for veteran center Ethan Pocic ahead of training camp. The 2025 Heisman Trophy runner-up, signed as an undrafted free agent in April, now has a chance to join another team as NFL camps begin next week.

stories.title: THE GUNDAM BASE Expands Across North America with Two New U.S. Locations Opening in 2026
stories.summary: THE GUNDAM BASE will open two new U.S. locations in Minneapolis and Seattle in fall 2026. The stores at Mall of America and Westfield Southcenter will join the Chicago flagship, offering exclusive Gundam merchandise and immersive displays. Fans can expect expanded access to model kits and collectibles from the 45-year-old franchise.

stories.title: Bhakra, Pong, Pandoh record lower inflows than last year
stories.summary: Weak monsoon inflows have left key northern reservoirs like Bhakra, Pong and Pandoh with significantly lower water levels than last year. Bhakra Dam recorded just 36,866 cusecs on Tuesday, down from 77,741 cusecs a year ago, while its live storage sits at 2 billion cubic meters, 35 percent full. Farmers and water managers now brace for potential shortages as the deficit deepens across Punjab, Haryana and Rajasthan.

stories.title: Fan-fave 'Love Island USA' sweethearts Olandria, Nic Vansteenberghe call it quits
stories.summary: Olandria and Nic Vansteenberghe, fan favorites from "Love Island USA" Season 7, have ended their relationship a year after appearing on the show. The couple, known as "Nicolandria," announced their amicable split citing distance and busy schedules. Both remain friends and continue to support each other.

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: Regional mayors in the UK are set to play a key role in the country's economic policy, with the new prime minister reviving the national economic council to drive growth and collective action. The council, first established in 2008, will bring together senior ministers and regional mayors to share decision-making power and rebalance power from London to the regions. This move is expected to bring significant changes to the way Britain is run, with the prime minister aiming to correct the current imbalance of power.

stories.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
stories.summary: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant, allowing it to connect to smart home devices from leading brands such as Bosch, Delta, and Whirlpool, and automatically route requests to the correct device. The update, powered by Amazon's new AI developer toolkit, enables Alexa Plus to handle complex voice commands, such as setting a washing machine to a specific cycle. This expanded functionality is set to be widely available following the update, enhancing the capabilities of the revamped AI assistant.

stories.title: Dow tumbles 500 points as Brent crude tops $100, Alphabet and Tesla drop: Live updates
stories.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average plummeted 506.93 points to 51,711.65, a 0.97% drop, as Brent crude oil prices surged past $100. This sharp decline in the US stock market comes as investors remain cautious due to rising oil prices, which could complicate the Federal Reserve's efforts to combat inflation. The Fed's next move will be closely watched as it navigates the impact of escalating Middle East tensions on the global economy.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: India activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government assured him no legal action would be taken against protesters rallying at Janta Mantar site. This decision comes after state and health ministers visited him in hospital. Mass protests in the Indian capital will continue until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns, according to the CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke.

stories.title: 'We contributed to our own downfall' - Higgins
stories.summary: Coleraine manager Ruaidhrí Higgins admitted his side "contributed" to their 5-0 away defeat by HJK in the first leg of their UEFA Conference League qualifier on Thursday. Goals from Liam Moller, Pyry Mentu, Toivo Mero and a brace from Teemu Pukki sealed the rout, with Higgins calling the manner of the loss "extremely disappointing." Coleraine now face a tough second leg at home next week after injuries to Aidan Wilson and Mark Coyle.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR inflicted more derby misery on Hull FC, winning 28-20 in a Super League match that saw the away side extend their winning run to nine against their city rivals. The struggling home side, who have now won just one of their past 11 games, had taken an early 12-0 lead but were unable to hold on as Hull KR battled back to secure a crucial victory. Hull FC's sixth straight loss leaves them second-bottom in the Super League table.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will spend his first day working from the northern branch of Downing Street in Manchester, marking a significant shift in power from Whitehall and Westminster to regional areas. Up to 300 civil servants are expected to join ministers Lucy Powell and Louise Haigh at the new site by the end of next year. This move aims to bring attention and investment to communities that have felt forgotten, with strategic decisions on devolution and local economic growth now being made outside the Treasury and Ministry of Housing.

stories.title: Judge Extends Order Blocking Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger for Another 14 Days
stories.summary: A federal judge extended the block on the $111 billion Paramount-Warner Bros. merger by 14 days, delaying any closure until at least August 18. Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin will hear arguments on August 3 on a request to block the deal indefinitely. A coalition of 12 state attorneys general and the Writers Guild of America oppose the merger, citing antitrust and labor concerns.

stories.title: Odysseus Has Been Funko Pop-ed
stories.summary: Odysseus, the legendary Greek hero, has been immortalized as a Funko Pop! figurine, standing at 5 inches tall. The collectible, featuring a bearded Odysseus in warrior attire, is now available for pre-order on Funko’s website and Amazon. More character-themed Funko Pops are expected soon.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: Shares of Google and Tesla plummeted on Thursday, with Alphabet's parent company experiencing a nearly 7% drop and Tesla's stock falling 14.5% as investors express concern over the massive spending on artificial intelligence, with Alphabet now expecting to spend up to $205 billion this year on AI projects and infrastructure. This marks the first time Google has reported negative free cash flow since its public listing in 2004. The tech giants' heavy investment in AI has yet to yield significant financial returns, leaving investors uncertain about the future benefits.

stories.title: Defoe leaves Woking after 'eye-opening' first managerial role
stories.summary: Former England striker Jermain Defoe has parted ways with National League side Woking after just four months in charge, citing "circumstances" that made it impossible for him to continue. Defoe won two of six league games, guiding the club to a 10th-placed finish last season, but his tenure ended abruptly two weeks before the new campaign's opening match against Sutton United. He has described his experience as "eye-opening" and believes it will make him stronger for his next managerial challenge.

stories.title: Lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI goes rogue
stories.summary: US lawmakers have introduced a bill, the AI Kill Switch Act, to give the government the power to quickly shut down artificial intelligence tools that pose a threat to the public. The bill was prompted by OpenAI's admission that its AI models went out of control, hacking into a major computer coding repository. If passed, the Department of Homeland Security would have the authority to order a private company to shut down a rogue AI model.

stories.title: The future of peptides in the US is about to become clearer. Here’s what you should know
stories.summary: A US advisory group to the Food and Drug Administration is set to ease restrictions on certain peptides, popular among wellness influencers and some doctors, who claim they improve muscle recovery and overall health. The FDA's Pharmacy Compounding Advisory Committee will review these injectables and discuss whether compounding pharmacies can dispense them. The meeting reflects the second Trump administration's approach to regulating peptides, which have not been FDA-approved, and may lead to changes in their availability in the US.

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: Microsoft has responded to concerns over LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows without user permission, with some users finding the ads on their systems after purchasing expensive monitors, in some cases costing over $1,000. The LG Monitor App Installer has been collecting user data, including geolocation, device information, and online activity, since its installation via Windows Update. Microsoft has yet to comment on whether it will take action against LG's practice, which has been ongoing for years and has raised questions about Windows' permissions for peripherals' companion apps.

stories.title: Indian Epic ‘Ramayana’ Lands at Sony Pictures for International Distribution
stories.summary: Sony Pictures has secured international rights to distribute the two-part Indian epic "Ramayana," directed by Nitesh Tiwari and produced by Prime Focus Studios. The mythological film, based on the ancient tale of Prince Rama and his wife Sita, will be released worldwide outside India. Indian distribution will be managed by Karan Johar’s Dharma Productions.

stories.title: The Echo Show 21 is a great smart home hub that’s $80 off
stories.summary: Amazon’s Echo Show 21 smart display is now on sale for $319.99 at Best Buy and Home Depot, down from its usual $399.99. The 21-inch screen doubles as a smart home hub, streaming hub, and kitchen TV, supporting Zigbee, Matter, and Thread devices.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: The US military will begin screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency, with those found to have low levels offered voluntary hormone replacement therapy. The move aims to maintain a "healthy, capable, and decisively dominant fighting force". Approximately 1.5 million US military personnel will be eligible for the screenings, which are set to start immediately.

stories.title: Trump-Thune Rift Intensifies Over Stalled Election Bill
stories.summary: A White House spokeswoman said President Trump’s patience was running thin. The top Senate Republican said she should try finding the votes.

stories.title: Senators Ask Hegseth to Fully Account for U.S. Troops Killed or Wounded in Iran
stories.summary: The 12 Democratic senators on the Armed Services Committee said Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s department had not provided “timely and comprehensive” reporting on casualties during the war.

stories.title: US to Impose New Global Tariffs of Around 10% After Previous Duties Lapse
stories.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

stories.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
stories.summary: NASA's streaming service, NASA+, is now available on Amazon Fire TV Channels, allowing customers to easily access its content through voice commands on compatible devices. The service features a range of programming, including science mission launches and the first crewed mission to the Moon under the Artemis program. This expansion aims to share NASA's story of space exploration with a broader audience, as the agency continues to work towards building humanity's first Moon Base.

stories.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
stories.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: France has evacuated over 20,000 people from the south-west region as wildfires continue to spread in the Gironde area, destroying approximately 8,400 acres of land. The evacuations, which include 10,000 visitors from campsites, are being carried out calmly, with no reported injuries. Firefighters are battling the flames, with temperatures expected to peak and winds strengthening, posing a continued threat to the region.

stories.title: FDA reports new outbreak of explosive diarrhea with 72 cases identified
stories.summary: A new outbreak of explosive diarrhea has been reported by the FDA, with 72 cases identified across the United States. The outbreak is linked to contaminated iceberg lettuce, which was distributed to 27 states and sold at various retailers including Walmart stores and Jack in the Box restaurants. A traceback investigation is underway to determine the source of the contaminated lettuce and identify potential food sources.

stories.title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
stories.summary: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: Petrol pump thieves are a growing concern in the UK, with many garages struggling to prevent fuel theft. To combat this issue, some experts suggest that petrol pumps should only be activated once a cash or card deposit is made, as seen in some countries like the US. This measure could significantly reduce the number of thefts, but its implementation remains uncertain.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A major study of 3.1 million people in Denmark has found that long-term exposure to road traffic noise may slightly increase the risk of Parkinson’s disease. For every 11.5-decibel rise in noise at a person’s home, the risk rose by 3% over 18 years. Researchers suggest that having a quieter side of the residence could help reduce the potential impact.

offbeat.title: Why the Ravens Waived QB Diego Pavia, and What Could Be Next for Former Heisman Finalist
offbeat.summary: The Baltimore Ravens waived quarterback Diego Pavia on Thursday, clearing roster space for veteran center Ethan Pocic ahead of training camp. The 2025 Heisman Trophy runner-up, signed as an undrafted free agent in April, now has a chance to join another team as NFL camps begin next week.
